meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Struggling to debug code? Learn how strategic portfolio building using Antigravity and Claude Code can transform your technical skills and career prospects.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of pattern recognition. By shifting your focus from 'fixing errors' to 'portfolio building,' you treat bugs as architectural lessons. Using tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can automate diagnostic workflows, turning every crash into a documented feature of your professional developer portfolio.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal overflowing with red text, the cursor blinking like a countdown to a deadline that feels impossible. You’ve refactored, you’ve googled, and yet, the logic remains elusive. The frustration isn't just about the code; it’s about the feeling that you’re stuck in a loop while the industry races ahead.

But here is the secret senior engineers know: the best way to master debugging is to stop viewing it as a chore and start viewing it as a design challenge. By focusing on portfolio building, you transform these roadblocks into case studies that prove your competency to future employers. It’s time to stop fighting your IDE and start engineering your career.

steps.title: Architectural Audit
steps.description: Before fixing, map the failure. Use Antigravity to visualize your data flow and identify where the state corruption occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase for architectural bottlenecks and state management inconsistencies that lead to runtime errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /review --focus 'architectural patterns and common failure points'

steps.title: Isolation Testing
steps.description: Isolate the buggy component to ensure your fix doesn't trigger a cascading failure elsewhere in the application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an isolated test suite for the component identified as the source of the crash, focusing on edge-case inputs.

steps.title: Documentation as Evidence
steps.description: Document the debugging process as a technical blog post or README entry for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize this debugging session into a professional technical write-up for a developer portfolio, highlighting the problem-solving methodology.

steps.title: Automated Remediation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to apply the fix and verify it against your new test suite.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the logic based on the identified root cause, ensuring type safety and performance optimization.

steps.title: Portfolio Integration
steps.description: Push the fix and the documentation to your public repository to showcase your growth to potential employers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a commit message that explains the technical challenge and the solution in a way that demonstrates senior-level thinking.
steps.claude_code_command: git commit -m 'feat: resolved runtime error in X; documented in portfolio case study'

pro_tips: Treat every bug as a 'post-mortem' document; employers value the ability to explain why something broke more than the ability to write perfect code.
Use Antigravity to generate sequence diagrams of your bugs—visual evidence of your logic is a massive portfolio multiplier.
Never fix a bug without writing an accompanying test case; it proves you understand regression prevention.
When using Claude Code, always ask it to 'explain the trade-offs' of its proposed fix to deepen your own conceptual understanding.
Build a 'Hall of Fame' folder in your repo containing the 5 most complex bugs you've solved; it’s a cheat sheet for technical interviews.
content.next_steps: Once your first case study is live, start contributing to one open-source project using these same debugging patterns to demonstrate collaboration skills.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs define your limits and start letting them define your expertise. Your portfolio is your ticket to the industry, and every resolved error is a badge of honor. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
